Legal

Privacy Policy.

Last updated: 11 March 2026

Thrive Bath Ltd, trading as Thrive Hyperbaric ("we", "us", "our"), respects your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, disclose and protect your personal data when you visit thrivehyperbaric.com (the "Site").

1. Data controller

Thrive Bath Ltd, trading as Thrive Hyperbaric, is the data controller (registered in England & Wales, company no. 15018698). You can contact us at hello@thrivehyperbaric.com.

2. Personal data we collect

  • Identity and contact information - name, email address, phone number.
  • Technical data - IP address, browser type, device information and pages visited (via cookies and analytics).
  • Marketing preferences - if you subscribe to our updates.

3. How we collect data

  • Directly from you, when you fill in a form or contact us.
  • Automatically, through cookies, server logs and analytics tools.

4. Purposes and lawful basis

We process your data under UK GDPR to:

  • provide and improve the Site (legitimate interests);
  • send marketing communications where you have consented;
  • comply with our legal obligations.

5. Sharing your data

We may share your data with:

  • service providers who assist with website hosting, analytics or email delivery, under strict contracts;
  • professional advisors or authorities, where legally required.

We do not sell your personal data.

6. International transfers

Data is primarily processed in the UK and EEA. Any transfers outside these areas are protected by appropriate safeguards, such as standard contractual clauses.

7. Data retention

We retain your personal data only for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes for which it was collected, or as required by law.

8. Your rights

Under UK GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict or object to the processing of your data, and to request its portability. You may also withdraw consent at any time. To exercise these rights, contact us. You can also complain to the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO).

9. Security

We use reasonable technical and organisational measures to protect your data. However, no system is completely secure.

10. Cookies and tracking

We use essential cookies for the Site to function, and analytics cookies (with your consent) to understand usage and improve the Site. See our Cookie Policy for details.

11. Changes

We may update this policy. Please check back regularly; significant changes will be notified on the Site or by email.
